clinical_history: 64-year-old man with increasing pedal edema. What is the ECG diagnosis?
diagnosis: Sinus rhythm with left atrial abnormality. Most striking are tall right precordial R waves with pathologic inferior and lateral Q waves giving a "reverse" type R wave progression. There are also ST-T abnormalities in I, aVL, V6. Thus the patient has coronary artery disease (CAD), s/p large infero-(postero)-lateral myocardial infarction (MI) accounting for congestive heart failure (CHF) findings.   Recall that differential diagnosis of tall right precordial R waves includes: 1) Normal/positional variants, 2) Right ventricular hypertrophy (RVH) (look for right axis deviation, P pulmonale),  3) Posterior/lateral MI (usually signs of inferior MI, too), 4) hypertrophic cardiomyopathy/idiopathic hypertrophic subaortic stenosis (HCM/IHSS), 5) right bundle branch block (RBBB), 6) Wolff-Parkinson-White (WPW ) variants with posterior/lateral pre-excitation 7) Duchenne muscular dystrophy (young men with myopathy).

report.findings: Left atrial abnormality; pathologic Q waves in inferior (II, III, aVF) and lateral (I, aVL, V6) leads; tall R waves in right precordial leads (V1, V2) indicating posterior involvement; 'reverse' R-wave progression; ST-T wave abnormalities in leads I, aVL, and V6.
report.impression: Large old infero-postero-lateral myocardial infarction, likely contributing to the patient's clinical signs of congestive heart failure.
